title: Cryo-EM Grid preparation
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ukti","text":"Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6hv0t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4t9b","text":"PI3KC3-C1~RAB1A sample with n-Octyl-β-D-Glucopyranoside (OG) at a final concentration of 0.05%","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a7osa","text":"QUANTIFOIL R 2/1 mesh Cu 300 holey carbon grids","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13jb0","text":"Vitrobot cryo-plunger (Thermo Fisher Scientific)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"339rv","text":"Cryo-EM microscope (300 kV Titan Krios with X-FEG and energy filter)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"29lta","text":"K3 Summit direct electron detector (Gatan)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"39lvc","text":"SerialEM software","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2dfpk","text":"Liquid nitrogen","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4hb7q","text":"Humidity control system","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Freshly glow-discharge the QUANTIFOIL R 2/1 mesh Cu 300 holey carbon grids using a glow-discharge system (PELCO easiGlow) at 25 mA current for 00:01:00 .
Mix the PI3KC3-C1~RAB1A sample with 0.05% n-Octyl-β-D-Glucopyranoside (OG) to achieve the desired final concentration.
Apply a small volume (typically 3-4 µL ) of the sample onto the glow-discharged side of the grids.
Load the grids with the sample into the Vitrobot cryo-plunger.
Set the Vitrobot to the following conditions:
Blot using a blotting force of -15 (may need calibration depending on your particular instrument) .
Prepare the Cryo-EM microscope (300 kV Titan Krios) for data acquisition.
Used X-FEG and an energy filter set to 20 eV.
Use SerialEM software for automated data collection.
Set the following parameters for data collection: Magnification: 81,000x. Super-resolution pixel size: 0.525 Å. Defocus range: -0.8 to -2.2 micrometers. Collect 50-frame image stacks.
Initiate the data collection process using the specified parameters.
Monitor the data acquisition progress and ensure that the images are being recorded.
Each movie has a cumulative electron dose of 50 e/Å².
creator: Annan SI Cook
